keyFindings.question: RQ1
keyFindings.finding: Launcher triple-lock ownership, dead-socket respawn, heartbeat self-shutdown, and idle cleanup close double-spawn/orphan risk for dual-stack delivery; D1 and D7 are acceptance-test deltas.

keyFindings.question: RQ2
keyFindings.finding: Warm Node/bridge process overhead measured under 50ms p95 on this host, fitting 3s hook ceilings when prompt-time hooks use warm-only timeout behavior.

keyFindings.question: RQ3
keyFindings.finding: Current launcher readiness is existence-only, so DD-001 source/dist freshness checking is required for the CLI shim or entrypoint.

keyFindings.question: RQ4
keyFindings.finding: The default macOS socket path is too long on this host, but current config pins /tmp/mk-spec-memory and bridge code supports TCP; D6 makes the CLI default and guard explicit.

keyFindings.question: RQ5
keyFindings.finding: OpenCode 1.16.2 lacks a documented local shell subcommand tool gate; plugin/Bash/MCP dual-stack remains viable and first-class shell-tool gating is upstream-only.

keyFindings.question: RQ6
keyFindings.finding: Scoped migration inventory found 93 unique files and 1041 references; full migration is a future packet, not a dual-stack blocker.

keyFindings.question: RQ7
keyFindings.finding: D1-D7 plus DD-001 cover the residual risk surface; no independent ninth delta is required.

keyFindings.question: RQ8
keyFindings.finding: All prior hedges have terminal classifications: methodology/artifact limitations accepted, estimates mitigated, OpenCode gate accepted upstream, migration/socket/latency risks bounded.

ruledOutDirections: Source-code implementation inside this research run
